classifications.phrase: The economic recovery in Australia has been stronger than was earlier expected
GDP is expected to decline in the September quarter
it is still expected to grow strongly again next year
the labour market has recovered faster than expected
the unemployment rate declining further to 4.9 per cent in June
there are reports of labour shortages in parts of the economy
The CPI inflation rate spiked to 3.8 per cent for the year to the June…
a pick-up in both wages growth and underlying inflation is expected
The Board will not increase the cash rate until actual inflation is sustainably within the…
this condition will not be met before 2024

payload.rationale: The statement signals a dovish stance overall, with the Board committing to maintain accommodative conditions and explicitly stating no rate increases until 2024, despite acknowledging stronger-than-expected recovery and labour market tightness.
